lesson: _sessions is an in-memory dict — any server restart loses all running sessions. Always persist status to disk atomically after every state change.
Use write-to-.tmp-then-os.replace() for JSON file writes. os.replace() is atomic on NTFS and POSIX — a crash mid-write leaves the old file intact, not a corrupt half-written one.
Always sanitise session IDs before using them in file paths. re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9]', '', sid) prevents path traversal attacks (e.g. sid='../../etc/passwd').
.claude/settings.local.json should never be committed to git — it contains local tool allowlists specific to one machine and is a minor info-leak if shared.
_req_log in auth.py accumulates IP keys forever if never cleaned up. Prune stale IPs (last request >120s ago) every 5 minutes inline inside check_rate_limit() — no background task needed in asyncio.

app/workspaces/ fills disk if completed sessions are never cleaned. Terminal sessions (complete/failed/killed/interrupted) should be GC'd after GC_MAX_AGE (default 4h). Also scan for orphan dirs not referenced by _sessions.
On Windows, asyncio.create_subprocess_exec requires the event loop to be ProactorEventLoop (default in Python 3.8+ on Windows). Never use SelectorEventLoop — it doesn't support subprocess pipes.
Railway terminates TLS and proxies requests. Do NOT add TrustedHostMiddleware restricting to 127.0.0.1 — it blocks all cloud traffic. Use ALLOWED_ORIGINS for CORS control instead.

The TAO engine in src/tao/ is stub scaffolding not used by the web server. All intelligence is delegated to 'claude -p' subprocess via stream-json output. Parse events: system, assistant (text+tool_use), tool_result, result.
claude -p subprocess mode requires Claude Code CLI in PATH. In cloud (Railway), Claude Code is NOT available — cloud deployments must use ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + SDK mode. Check ANALYSIS_MODE env var.

CLAUDE.md is loaded into every future Claude session. Keeping it accurate and detailed (file map, conventions, smoke tests) eliminates all ramp-up time for AI sessions and is the highest single-leverage documentation fix.

Pi Dev Ops is a split-screen companion: Left = Claude Desktop CLI writing code; Right = Pi Dev Ops orchestrating, tracking, and pushing to Linear. It is NOT a standalone IDE. Every architectural decision should serve this companion model.
All Priority 1+2 items (13 issues) completed in a single overnight session. Pattern: full-clearance sprint with no authority gates produces 10x throughput vs issue-by-issue execution.
ZTE score moved 35→50 in two sprints (P1+P2). The highest leverage actions were: CLAUDE.md population (+4 pts total across sessions), evaluator tier (+2), PITER+ADW (+4), webhook triggers (+2).
MCP SDK subpath imports are required: '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js' and '.../server/stdio.js'. The top-level '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' import does not export McpServer directly.

Webhook HMAC verification must use hmac.compare_digest() (timing-safe). Both GitHub (x-hub-signature-256: sha256=<hex>) and Linear (Linear-Signature: <hex>) use HMAC-SHA256 but with different header formats.

The sandbox IS the workspace directory (app/workspaces/{session_id}/). Each session gets an isolated clone. Sandbox enforcement = verify workspace exists before Phase 4 and refuse to run claude -p outside it.
Smoke test lesson — safe to delete
Smoke test lesson — safe to delete
Smoke test lesson — safe to delete

When wrapping claude_agent_sdk.ClaudeSDKClient, use the public `async for message in client.receive_response()` loop — NOT `client._query.receive_messages()`. The latter is a private API used in telegram-bot/src/claude/sdk_integration.py line 405 that will break on SDK upgrades. Public loop is stable.

Test SDK-wrapped functions by patching `claude_agent_sdk.ClaudeSDKClient` with unittest.mock.AsyncMock. Set `return_value.__aenter__.return_value.receive_response` to an async iterator yielding mock message objects with `.content` attributes. Never test against the real API in unit tests.

Pi-SEO scan detected 6 exposed keys in docs/runbooks and scripts/ files across dr-nrpg, synthex, ccw-crm (2026-04-10). Most are in documentation, not production code, but must still be verified as example values not real keys. Add pre-commit detect-secrets hook to every portfolio repo before Pi-SEO activation.

Parallel agent dispatch beats sequential by ~8x for independent tasks. Dispatch multiple Agent tool calls in a single message rather than waiting for each to return. Constraint: agents must not share target files (partition by file ownership).

Writing charters and plans without first reading the real system state produces orphan work. Always run a reconnaissance swarm against the actual .harness/ state BEFORE drafting new project plans. The 2026-04-11 Pi-SEO swarm lost ~2 hours to a parallel /Pi-CEO/Pi-SEO/ folder that duplicated existing .harness/ content.

Anthropic docs URLs (docs.claude.com) redirect to platform.claude.com and code.claude.com. Any httpx fetcher must set follow_redirects=True. Filename collisions can occur if URLs are keyed by last path segment alone — use two-segment extraction or full URL hash.

Telegram push from any sandboxed Python environment needs only TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN + a chat_id (first entry in ALLOWED_USERS works). Full python-telegram-bot package is NOT required — urllib + POST to api.telegram.org/bot{token}/sendMessage is sufficient. See scripts/send_telegram.py.

Long-running autonomous harnesses MUST pre-grant every tool permission at three layers: (1) .claude/settings.json with permissions.defaultMode=bypassPermissions and an explicit allow list, (2) ClaudeAgentOptions(permission_mode='bypassPermissions') for every SDK call site, (3) --dangerously-skip-permissions in every subprocess claude -p invocation via a shared CLAUDE_EXTRA_FLAGS config const. Missing any layer means a cron job can stall indefinitely at 3 AM waiting for a click that never comes.

Scheduled-tasks MCP runs inside the desktop Claude session and does NOT inherit the repo .claude/settings.json allowlist. Shrink every scheduled task prompt to a single shell command calling a standalone Python helper — this minimises tool-approval surface to Bash alone, approvable with one Run now click. Never let a scheduled task prompt ask Claude to read files, compose text, and push messages as separate steps — each step is another potential prompt.

Pi-SEO cron scheduler silent regression root cause: (1) cron-triggers.json last_fired_at resets to git-committed values on Railway redeploy, causing missed windows between deploys. (2) _matches() debounce used (time.time() - last) < 90 without abs(), making bogus future timestamps block all subsequent fires. Fix: abs() in debounce, startup catch-up (fire overdue triggers within 10s of boot), 30-min watchdog loop creates Urgent Linear ticket if no scan in 12h.

Scheduled-tasks MCP runs each task inside a fresh Cowork sandbox at /sessions/<random-id>/mnt/<folder>. The session ID changes every run, so hardcoding the user's local Mac path in a task prompt will fail on every execution. Always discover the repo dynamically with find /sessions -type d -name <repo> at the top of the task prompt, then cd into it. The heartbeat task proved this by self-recovering on first failure — assistant inside the scheduled task updated its own prompt and sent message ID 31.

Bidirectional Telegram loop: poller (5min, getUpdates with offset file) -> .harness/telegram-inbox/<update_id>.json -> watchdog drain (30min) -> route (fix/brief/idea/note) -> ack in next envelope. Inbound ideas are NEVER auto-promoted to Linear; they land in .harness/ideas-from-phone/ for human review. Green runs stay silent EXCEPT when inbox traffic exists - that guarantees confirmation without battery spam.

Silent failure mode discovered: Pi-Dev-Ops Railway production /health reports ok but autonomy.py linear_todo_poller skips every 5-min cycle when LINEAR_API_KEY env var is missing. The health endpoint surfaces claude_cli and anthropic_key but NOT linear_api_key — so a missing key looks identical to a working system except sessions.total stays at 0. Symptom: 0 build sessions created since last Railway restart, yet health green. Fix: add LINEAR_API_KEY to Railway env vars. Permanent fix: surface linear_api_key:bool in /health response so the next watchdog iteration can catch this from outside the Railway network.

Sleep-first poller loops have a hidden bootstrap-delay bug. `while True: await asyncio.sleep(interval)` looks harmless but after a Railway restart the first fetch is delayed by the full interval (5 minutes for the Linear poller). Combined with a silent skip when LINEAR_API_KEY is missing, this made Pi-Dev-Ops appear stuck for five full minutes on every cold start — long enough that a human always assumed the rails had died and manually prompted the system. Fix: do-while pattern with a short startup_delay (10s default, TAO_AUTONOMY_STARTUP_DELAY override). Also: surface autonomy.armed = enabled AND linear_key_ok in /health so the watchdog can detect the silent-failure mode instead of reporting ok on a dead system. Always log every skipped poll, not just the first one.

/health endpoints should report the state of the WORK the service is supposed to do, not just whether the process is breathing. Pi-Dev-Ops /health was returning 200 ok with uptime_s climbing nicely while the autonomy poller was silently skipping every cycle because LINEAR_API_KEY wasn't set in Railway. The watchdog trusted /health and reported green. Lesson: every long-running background loop needs two things in /health: (1) a boolean that says 'this loop is armed and will actually do work on the next tick', and (2) a timestamp/counter showing the last successful tick. Without both, you get silent success theatre.

The first overnight autonomous attempt failed because the rails depended on Cowork being open on Phill's Mac. Scheduled-tasks MCP runs in ephemeral Cowork sandboxes — when the Mac sleeps or Cowork exits, every cron entry goes silent. Railway is the only 24/7 component in our stack, and it was running stale code because seven commits sat unpushed on local main (the sandbox has no SSH key). Lesson: 'autonomous' is a property of TOPOLOGY, not of how clever the code is. If the always-on path doesn't include every component that needs to run while the human sleeps, the system isn't autonomous — it's a cron job in someone's editor. Move work to Railway, use GH Actions as the test-truth source, webhook Telegram inbound, and give the build pipeline its own push credentials. See ARCHITECTURE-V2.md.

The marathon watchdog ran pytest in a fresh Cowork scheduled-task sandbox every 30 minutes. That sandbox's package set is NOT guaranteed to match production or the dev sandbox. The anthropic>=0.90 dependency declared in pyproject.toml was missing from the scheduled-task sandbox, so imports blew up, pytest exited 1, and the watchdog escalated CRITICAL to Telegram at 00:38 UTC on 2026-04-12. Tests were actually 46/46 green. A watchdog that cries wolf destroys its own usefulness — after one false CRITICAL alert, every subsequent alert is suspect. Lesson: NEVER escalate CRITICAL from an environment you don't control. Fixes: (a) watchdog uses `pytest --collect-only` not a full run, so imports are exercised without runtime creds, (b) ModuleNotFoundError downgraded to warn with explicit 'sandbox env issue, not a real test failure' message, (c) real test-green truth comes from GH Actions in a known-good environment, not this watchdog.

Phill asked about Google's TurboQuant (KV cache quantization) as a memory solution. TurboQuant compresses transformer attention internals on CUDA GPUs to stretch LLM context windows. Pi-CEO's memory problem is different: selecting and condensing relevant business context per per-project agent session. Pi-CEO runs against Anthropic's API (we don't own the model), on CPU-only Railway (no CUDA), and our bottleneck is relevance not raw memory. The right answer is semantic RAG: per-project memory/ folder, retrieval step before session start, weekly summarization of long histories, embedding compression as a later optimization. TurboQuant's rotate-then-quantize trick is actually useful for embedding compression eventually — same intuition, different layer. See TURBOQUANT-ASSESSMENT.md for the full writeup and the four-piece implementation plan.

In Railway deployments, request.client.host is the internal load-balancer IP which may vary per LB instance. A per-IP rate limiter keyed on request.client.host never fills up. Trust X-Forwarded-For in cloud environments (RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT set) — Railway strips any client-supplied XFF before injecting the real client IP so this cannot be spoofed.

The claude CLI sets ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="" in the parent shell env as a security measure. Any service launched from that shell (telegram-bot, Python SDKs) inherits the empty string and sends it as x-api-key, causing HTTP 401. Fix: call os.environ.pop("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", None) before delegating to the Claude SDK when no valid key is configured so the subprocess picks up its own OAuth tokens.

1Password secret references (op://vault/item/field) in .env files are only resolved when the process is launched via . Python dotenv reads them as literal strings. Add a Pydantic field_validator that treats op:// values and empty strings as None so they fall through to fallback auth rather than passing an obviously-invalid credential to an API client.

When a Next.js API route (Vercel) uses process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY directly (not via makeClient which trims), a trailing newline in the Vercel-stored key causes HTTP 401. Always call .trim() on env-sourced API keys before passing to the Anthropic SDK. This applies to any env var that goes into an HTTP Authorization header.

When removing a hardcoded fallback secret (e.g. ADMIN_JWT_SECRET default value in code), the CI build step immediately fails because the variable was previously silently injected. Fix requires two steps: (1) add the secret to GitHub repo via 'gh secret set ADMIN_JWT_SECRET', (2) inject it in the workflow build step env block. Removing a fallback in code without updating CI secrets causes a hard CI failure on the next push.

GitHub Actions dependency-review-action@v4 fails with 'Dependency review is not supported on this repository' when the repo's Dependency Graph is not yet indexed. This is not a package license/vulnerability issue — it is a repo configuration issue. Fix: enable vulnerability alerts via 'gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/vulnerability-alerts -X PUT'. Interim: add continue-on-error: true to the job so it doesn't block merges while the graph indexes. NPM Audit + Trivy provide equivalent coverage.

A stale Vercel project rootDirectory config (e.g. 'apps/web' set from a monorepo that was later flattened) causes build failures with 'directory not found'. The UI setting is unreliable for clearing this. Use the Vercel API: PATCH /v9/projects/{projectId} with body {"rootDirectory": null} and header Authorization: Bearer <token>. Get projectId from .vercel/project.json in the repo.

Before trying to merge a PR, always check branch protection required status checks: 'gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/branches/main/protection --jq .required_status_checks'. A failing CI job that is NOT in the required_status_checks list does not block the merge. Only checks listed there are gates. Dependency Review, E2E tests, and accessibility tests are typically advisory-only.

Health check scripts that alert on first failure cause false positives from normal service restarts (e.g. n8n Docker container restart takes ~10s). Add a consecutive-failure threshold (2 failures) with a state file that persists between runs. Also add a cooldown period (30 min) between repeated alerts for the same service to prevent alert fatigue. State file path: ~/pi-ceo/logs/health_check_state.json.

A macOS LaunchAgent plist that launches cloudflared must explicitly include the tunnel subcommand args: ['cloudflared', 'tunnel', 'run', '<tunnel-name>']. Without the 'tunnel run <name>' args, cloudflared starts but does not route any traffic — the service appears running but the tunnel is inactive. After editing the plist, reload with: launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/<label>.plist && launchctl load ~/Library/LaunchAgents/<label>.plist.

When using 'gh pr merge' to merge a PR with branch protection, use '--merge' flag (not '--auto'). The '--auto' flag requires the repo to have 'enablePullRequestAutoMerge' setting enabled (org-level setting). '--merge' triggers an immediate merge attempt and returns a clear error if required status checks are still pending, making the failure visible and actionable.
